 {"id":"bd-905b","title":"Define collision handling policy","description":"**Clear policy for path collision handling**

## What
Define what happens when collision is detected (different repos -> same path).

## Why
Current plan detects collisions but doesn't specify the behavior.

## Policy Options
1. **Warn and skip second repo** (safest, current implicit behavior)
2. **Fail fast** (strict, stops processing)
3. **Prompt user** (interactive only)
4. **Auto-rename with suffix** (risky)

## Chosen Policy: Warn and skip
```
Collision detected:
  Path: /home/user/projects/utils
  Configured: org1/utils (will be synced)
  Skipped:    org2/utils (same path)

To fix: Change layout to 'owner-repo' in config:
  ru config --set LAYOUT=owner-repo
```

## Implementation
- First occurrence wins
- Log warning with resolution hint
- Include in summary as 'skipped:collision'
- Exit code 2 (conflicts) if any collisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear messaging about collision
- First-wins policy
- Resolution hint in output
- Documented in README","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.858988726-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.890572977-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9j92","title":"Implement main dashboard view (ntm mode)","description":"Task: Implement Main Dashboard View

Purpose
-------
Full-screen TUI dashboard showing all pending questions, active sessions,
and summary statistics in a organized layout.

Layout Design
-------------
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ru review                                    Progress: 5/8  Runtime  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                       |
|  PENDING QUESTIONS (3)                                                |
|  -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  [1] ● project-alpha     Issue #42     Priority: CRITICAL             |
|      Context: Authentication failing on Windows                       |
|      > a) Quick fix  b) Full refactor  c) Skip                       |
|                                                                       |
|  [2] ○ project-beta      PR #15        Priority: NORMAL              |
|      [Press Enter to expand]                                          |
|                                                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ACTIVE SESSIONS                                                      |
|  -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  Repo              State         Progress      Health                 |
|  project-delta     GENERATING    80%           Healthy                |
|  project-epsilon   THINKING      60%           Healthy                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  SUMMARY                                                              |
|  Completed: 4 | Issues: 7 | PRs: 1 | Commits: 12                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [1-9] Answer [Enter] Expand [d] Drill [s] Skip [a] Apply [q] Quit    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

Implementation
--------------

Dashboard State
  declare -A DASHBOARD_STATE=(
    [selected_index]=0
    [expanded_question]=""
    [scroll_offset]=0
    [panel_focus]="questions"
  )

render_dashboard()
  clear
  render_header
  render_questions_panel
  render_sessions_panel
  render_summary_panel
  render_footer

render_questions_panel()
  Load questions from queue
  For each question:
    if expanded: show full context
    else: show one-line summary
  Highlight selected with ANSI

render_sessions_panel()
  Query session states
  Format as table with columns

render_summary_panel()
  Load metrics from state
  Format as single line stats

Dashboard Loop
--------------
run_dashboard()
  while true:
    render_dashboard
    read -rsn1 key
    handle_keypress "$key"
    
    # Check for new events
    poll_events

handle_keypress()
  case key:
    1-9: select_and_answer
    Enter: toggle_expand
    d: open_drilldown
    s: skip_current
    q: confirm_quit

Refresh Logic
-------------
- Full refresh every 5 seconds
- Immediate refresh on user action
- Poll for new questions/events

Terminal Handling
-----------------
- Save cursor position
- Use alternate screen buffer
- Restore on exit
- Handle resize (SIGWINCH)

Testing
-------
- Verify layout renders correctly
- Verify keyboard navigation
- Verify refresh updates data
- Verify resize handling
- Test with different terminal sizes

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Dashboard renders cleanly
- [ ] Questions panel shows all pending
- [ ] Sessions panel updates in real-time
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work
- [ ] Handles terminal resize","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:43.609323436-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:43.609323436-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9j92","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:08:34.753044804-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-9s7y","title":"Implement local driver (tmux + stream-json)","description":"# Task: Implement Local Driver (tmux + stream-json)

## Purpose
Implement the session driver interface using tmux and Claude's stream-json output mode. This is the fallback driver when ntm is not available.

## Background
Claude Code supports --output-format stream-json which emits NDJSON events:
- System init with session_id and available tools
- Assistant messages with text and tool_use
- Tool results
- Final result with status

We capture this output and parse it for question detection.

## Implementation

### local_driver_start_session()
```bash
local_driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    
    # Create named pipe for event streaming
    rm -f "$event_pipe"
    mkfifo "$event_pipe"
    
    # Build claude command
    local claude_cmd="claude -p $(printf '%q' "$prompt") --output-format stream-json"
    
    # Create tmux session
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_name" -c "$wt_path" \
        "exec $claude_cmd 2>&1 | tee '$log_file' > '$event_pipe'"
    
    # Return session info
    cat << EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_name",
  "worktree": "$wt_path",
  "log_file": "$log_file",
  "event_pipe": "$event_pipe",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_send_to_session()
```bash
local_driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
    
    # Send via tmux
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" "$message" Enter
    
    return $?
}
```

### local_driver_get_session_state()
```bash
local_driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Check if tmux session exists
    if ! tmux has-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo '{"state": "complete", "reason": "session_ended"}'
        return
    fi
    
    # Capture last lines of output
    local output
    output=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$session_id" -p -S -50 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Detect state from output
    local state="generating"
    local wait_reason=""
    
    # Check for prompt (waiting)
    if echo "$output" | grep -qE '^\s*>\s*$|Press Enter|Select.*:|Enter.*:'; then
        state="waiting"
        wait_reason="prompt_detected"
    fi
    
    # Check for errors
    if echo "$output" | grep -qiE 'error:|panic:|rate.limit|429'; then
        state="error"
    fi
    
    cat << EOF
{
  "state": "$state",
  "wait_reason": "$wait_reason",
  "session_id": "$session_id"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_stream_events()
```bash
local_driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    local wt_path="$3"
    
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    
    # Parse stream-json events
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        
        # Parse JSON event type
        local event_type
        event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // empty' 2>/dev/null)
        
        case "$event_type" in
            system)
                local session_id_from_event
                session_id_from_event=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.session_id // empty')
                $callback "init" "$session_id_from_event"
                ;;
            assistant)
                # Check for AskUserQuestion tool_use
                local tool_name
                tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r \
                    '.message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use") | .name // empty' 2>/dev/null)
                
                if [[ "$tool_name" == "AskUserQuestion" ]]; then
                    local question_data
                    question_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c \
                        '.message.content[] | select(.name=="AskUserQuestion") | .input')
                    $callback "question" "$question_data"
                fi
                ;;
            result)
                local status
                status=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.status // "unknown"')
                $callback "complete" "$status"
                break
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$event_pipe"
}
```

### local_driver_stop_session()
```bash
local_driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Kill tmux session
    tmux kill-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    return 0
}
```

### local_driver_interrupt_session()
```bash
local_driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Send Ctrl+C
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" C-c
    
    return 0
}
```

## Session Monitoring Loop
```bash
local_driver_monitor_sessions() {
    local -a sessions=("$@")
    
    while [[ ${#sessions[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do
        local -a active_sessions=()
        
        for session_id in "${sessions[@]}"; do
            local state
            state=$(local_driver_get_session_state "$session_id" | jq -r '.state')
            
            case "$state" in
                complete)
                    handle_session_complete "$session_id"
                    ;;
                error)
                    handle_session_error "$session_id"
                    ;;
                waiting)
                    handle_session_waiting "$session_id"
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
                *)
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
            esac
        done
        
        sessions=("${active_sessions[@]}")
        sleep 2
    done
}
```

## Testing
- Launch session, verify tmux created
- Send message, verify delivered
- Detect AskUserQuestion event
- Detect session completion
- Interrupt and stop work correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions launch in tmux with stream-json
- [ ] Events parsed from NDJSON stream
- [ ] AskUserQuestion detected
- [ ] Messages sent via tmux send-keys
- [ ] Session cleanup works
- [ ] Works without ntm installed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.53945729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.53945729-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9s7y","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.79309431-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9xjc","title":"Phase 6: Error Handling and Recovery","description":"Phase 6: Error Handling and Recovery

Overview
--------
Robust error handling, retry strategies, checkpointing, and recovery
mechanisms to handle the many failure modes in a multi-session system.

Why Critical?
-------------
Many things can fail:
- Network timeouts
- API rate limits (GitHub, Anthropic)
- Session crashes
- User interrupts (Ctrl+C)
- Disk full
- Authentication expiry

Without proper handling, partial work is lost.

Components
----------

6.1 Error Categories
   Classify errors for appropriate handling:
   - Transient: retry with backoff
   - Rate Limit: backoff + rotate
   - Auth: alert user, pause
   - Session: restart, preserve state
   - User: graceful handling
   - Fatal: abort with clear message

6.2 Retry with Exponential Backoff
   For transient failures:
   - Base delay with exponential growth
   - Jitter to prevent thundering herd
   - Max attempts limit
   - Configurable per operation

6.3 Checkpointing
   Periodic state saves for recovery:
   - After each significant operation
   - Includes all necessary context
   - Atomic writes prevent corruption
   - Configurable interval

6.4 Resume from Checkpoint
   Continue interrupted reviews:
   - Load checkpoint state
   - Identify completed/pending work
   - Skip already-done repos
   - Restore question queue

6.5 Graceful Degradation
   When advanced features unavailable:
   - ntm missing: use local driver
   - gum missing: use ANSI fallback
   - Tests fail: continue without push
   - Rate limited: reduce parallelism

6.6 Signal Handling
   Proper cleanup on interrupts:
   - SIGINT (Ctrl+C): save state, exit
   - SIGTERM: save state, exit
   - SIGHUP: continue in background?
   - Set SYNC_INTERRUPTED flag

Exit Criteria
-------------
- All error categories handled
- Retry logic works correctly
- Checkpoints saved periodically
- Resume continues from checkpoint
- Graceful degradation works
- Signals handled cleanly

Estimated Effort
----------------
~300 lines of Bash","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:59.930455089-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:59.930455089-05:00"}

@@ -1370,8 +1370,20 @@ ru uses it to:
 - Compute metrics without transcript scraping
 - Audit what was done
 
+**Location (absolute):**
+```
+{worktree}/.ru/review-plan.json
+```
+
 **Schema (v1):**
 
+Top-level contract notes:
+- `schema_version` is an integer and must be `1` for this version.
+- `repo` is `owner/repo` (GitHub shorthand).
+- `worktree_path` is the absolute path of the isolated worktree for this repo.
+- `items` must exist (may be empty if nothing was reviewed).
+- `metadata` must exist (at minimum timestamps + model/driver info).
+
 ```json
 {
   "schema_version": 1,
@@ -1387,7 +1399,9 @@ ru uses it to:
       "priority": "high",
       "decision": "fix",
       "notes": "Root cause: path separator in auth.py:234",
-      "risk_level": "low"
+      "risk_level": "low",
+      "files_changed": ["src/auth.py"],
+      "lines_changed": 5
     },
     {
       "type": "pr",
@@ -1411,21 +1425,29 @@ ru uses it to:
       ],
       "recommended": "Quick fix",
       "answered": true,
-      "answer": "Quick fix"
+      "answer": "Quick fix",
+      "answered_at": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
     }
   ],
 
   "git": {
     "branch": "ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo",
     "base_ref": "main",
     "commits": [
-      {"sha": "abc123", "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py"}
+      {
+        "sha": "abc123def456",
+        "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py",
+        "files": ["src/auth.py"],
+        "insertions": 3,
+        "deletions": 2
+      }
     ],
     "tests": {
       "ran": true,
       "ok": true,
       "command": "make test",
-      "output_summary": "12 tests passed"
+      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
+      "duration_seconds": 45
     }
   },
 
@@ -1440,6 +1462,11 @@ ru uses it to:
       "target": "issue#42",
       "reason": "completed"
     },
+    {
+      "op": "label",
+      "target": "issue#42",
+      "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]
+    },
     {
       "op": "comment",
       "target": "pr#15",
@@ -1451,29 +1478,156 @@ ru uses it to:
     "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
     "completed_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
     "duration_seconds": 900,
-    "context_usage_percent": 45
+    "context_usage_percent": 45,
+    "model": "claude-sonnet-4",
+    "driver": "local"
   }
 }
 ```
 
+**Field definitions**
+
+Top level:
+| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
+|-------|------|----------|------|
+| `schema_version` | int | yes | Must be `1` |
+| `run_id` | string | yes | Unique run identifier for the overall review run |
+| `repo` | string | yes | `owner/repo` |
+| `worktree_path` | string | yes | Absolute path to isolated worktree |
+| `items` | array | yes | Work items reviewed (may be empty) |
+| `questions` | array | no | Questions asked/answered (optional) |
+| `git` | object | no | Git summary (optional; absent if no commits) |
+| `gh_actions` | array | no | GitHub mutations to apply later (optional; Plan mode must not execute them) |
+| `metadata` | object | yes | Session metadata (timing + model/driver) |
+
+Item object:
+| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
+|-------|------|----------|------|
+| `type` | `"issue"`\|`"pr"` | yes | Work item type |
+| `number` | int | yes | Issue/PR number |
+| `title` | string | yes | Title as seen on GitHub |
+| `priority` | string | yes | Suggested values: `critical` \| `high` \| `normal` \| `low` |
+| `decision` | string | yes | `fix` \| `skip` \| `needs-info` \| `closed` |
+| `notes` | string | no | Rationale / summary |
+| `risk_level` | string | no | Suggested values: `low` \| `medium` \| `high` \| `n/a` |
+| `files_changed` | array | no | Files touched for this item (best-effort) |
+| `lines_changed` | int | no | Net lines changed (best-effort) |
+
+Question object:
+| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
+|-------|------|----------|------|
+| `id` | string | yes | Unique within plan file |
+| `prompt` | string | yes | Question text |
+| `options` | array | no | Options the user can choose from |
+| `recommended` | string | no | Agent recommendation |
+| `answered` | bool | yes | Whether answered during this run |
+| `answer` | string | no | Required when `answered=true` |
+| `answered_at` | string | no | ISO-8601 timestamp; required when `answered=true` |
+
+`gh_actions` object:
+| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
+|-------|------|----------|------|
+| `op` | string | yes | `comment` \| `close` \| `label` \| `merge` |
+| `target` | string | yes | `issue#N` or `pr#N` |
+| `body` | string | no | Required for `comment` |
+| `reason` | string | no | Required for `close` (e.g. `completed`, `not_planned`) |
+| `labels` | array | no | Required for `label` |
+
 **Validation:**
 ```bash
 validate_review_plan() {
     local plan_file="$1"
 
+    # Must exist
+    [[ -f "$plan_file" ]] || { echo "Plan file not found: $plan_file" >&2; return 1; }
+
+    # We require jq for validation. ru can treat jq as optional for other flows,
+    # but review-plan.json is an Apply-phase contract and must be machine-validated.
+    command -v jq &>/dev/null || { echo "jq is required to validate review plans" >&2; return 3; }
+
     # Must be valid JSON
-    jq empty "$plan_file" || return 1
+    jq empty "$plan_file" >/dev/null || { echo "Invalid JSON" >&2; return 1; }
 
     # Must have required fields
-    jq -e '.schema_version and .repo and .items' "$plan_file" >/dev/null || return 1
+    jq -e '
+      (.schema_version == 1)
+      and (.run_id | type == "string" and length > 0)
+      and (.repo | type == "string" and length > 0)
+      and (.worktree_path | type == "string" and length > 0)
+      and (.items | type == "array")
+      and (.metadata | type == "object")
+    ' "$plan_file" >/dev/null || { echo "Missing/invalid required top-level fields" >&2; return 1; }
 
     # Items must have required fields
-    jq -e '.items | all(.type and .number and .decision)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null || return 1
+    jq -e '
+      .items
+      | all(
+          (.type | IN("issue","pr"))
+          and (.number | type == "number" and . > 0)
+          and (.title | type == "string" and length > 0)
+          and (.decision | IN("fix","skip","needs-info","closed"))
+        )
+    ' "$plan_file" >/dev/null || { echo "One or more items are missing required fields" >&2; return 1; }
+
+    # Questions (if present) must have required fields, and answered questions must include answer + answered_at
+    jq -e '
+      if has("questions") then
+        (.questions | type == "array")
+        and (.questions
+          | all(
+              (.id | type == "string" and length > 0)
+              and (.prompt | type == "string" and length > 0)
+              and (.answered | type == "boolean")
+              and (if .answered then
+                    (.answer | type == "string" and length > 0)
+                    and (.answered_at | type == "string" and length > 0)
+                  else true end)
+            )
+        )
+      else true end
+    ' "$plan_file" >/dev/null || { echo "Invalid questions section" >&2; return 1; }
+
+    # gh_actions (if present) must have required fields and op-specific fields
+    jq -e '
+      if has("gh_actions") then
+        (.gh_actions | type == "array")
+        and (.gh_actions
+          | all(
+              (.op | IN("comment","close","label","merge"))
+              and (.target | type == "string" and length > 0)
+              and (if .op == "comment" then (.body | type == "string" and length > 0) else true end)
+              and (if .op == "close" then (.reason | type == "string" and length > 0) else true end)
+              and (if .op == "label" then (.labels | type == "array") else true end)
+            )
+        )
+      else true end
+    ' "$plan_file" >/dev/null || { echo "Invalid gh_actions section" >&2; return 1; }
 
     return 0
 }
 ```
 
+**Summary helper (for logs / TUI):**
+```bash
+summarize_review_plan() {
+    local plan_file="$1"
+    command -v jq &>/dev/null || { echo "jq is required" >&2; return 3; }
+
+    jq -r '
+      def c(decision): ([.items[]? | select(.decision == decision)] | length);
+      "Repository: \(.repo)",
+      "Items reviewed: \(.items | length)",
+      "  - Fixed: \(c("fix"))",
+      "  - Skipped: \(c("skip"))",
+      "  - Needs info: \(c("needs-info"))",
+      "  - Closed: \(c("closed"))",
+      "Commits: \((.git.commits // []) | length)",
+      "Tests: \(if (.git.tests.ok // false) then "PASS" else "FAIL/NOT RUN" end)",
+      "gh_actions pending: \((.gh_actions // []) | length)"
+    ' "$plan_file"
+}
+```
+
